title: SK Hynix debuts on Wall Street with a $26.5 billion offering
summary: The South Korean semiconductor company completed its public offering in the United States with demand exceeding expectations.

title: Wall Street closes in the green after Trump's contacts with Iran
summary: The main U.S. indices rose amid the possibility of a de-escalation in the Middle East conflict.

title: Samsung seeks to meet with Nvidia amid the rise of artificial intelligence
summary: The announcement of a possible alliance between Samsung and Nvidia and DeepSeek's advances boost the chip sector.

title: The Fed creates working groups with Marc Andreessen and former Walmart director
summary: The U.S. central bank appointed five groups of experts to oversee its monetary and regulatory policies.

title: Goldman Sachs bans financial bets for its employees
summary: The financial institution tightened its internal rules amid the growth of regulated betting platforms.

title: The Strait of Hormuz on the verge of collapse sends oil prices soaring
summary: US attacks on Iran disrupted maritime traffic in the strait, raising crude oil prices.

title: Fast Retailing falls on the stock market despite improving profit forecasts
summary: The operator of Uniqlo posted positive results but warned about the impact of the yen on its accounts.

title: Costco leads the Nasdaq losses, falling 4.2%
summary: The U.S. retailer suffered its worst day in months due to concerns about consumer spending.

title: Fed minutes show divisions on the future of rates
summary: The central bank maintains a cautious tone while committee members differ on the direction of rates.

title: The Ibex 35 bounces back driven by the de-escalation between the U.S. and Iran
summary: The Spanish benchmark regained ground after previous losses due to the geopolitical conflict.

title: Meta accelerates its bet on AI with new models and its own chips
summary: Mark Zuckerberg's company allocates $145 billion to infrastructure to compete in artificial intelligence.

title: Micron will invest $250 billion in the U.S. until 2035
summary: The U.S. chipmaker is expanding its production capacity amid strong demand for semiconductors for AI.

title: Asian stock markets rise, buoyed by Wall Street optimism
summary: The markets of Japan, China, Hong Kong, Australia, and Indonesia opened higher, following Wall Street.

title: OpenAI's number two, Fidji Simo, leaves her position for health reasons
summary: Fidji Simo, OpenAI's director of applications, steps down to a part-time role to treat an illness.

title: The German DAX closes above 25,000 points
summary: The German index reached a new record driven by the technology sector and geopolitical calm.

title: The European Parliament gives the green light to the digital euro
summary: The European chamber approved the creation of an ECB digital currency, pending final regulation.

title: The yen strengthens to 161.90 per dollar after comments from the Finance Minister
summary: The Japanese Finance Minister intervened verbally to stem the yen's depreciation.

title: Kim Jong-un orders strengthening of North Korea's foreign intelligence
summary: The North Korean leader strengthens his espionage agency to improve operations abroad.

title: Russian Education Minister visits Kuril Islands amid Japanese protest
summary: The visit of a senior Russian official to the disputed islands reignites diplomatic tension with Japan.

title: Japan promotes female entrepreneurship with multiple regional initiatives
summary: Several Japanese prefectures launch mentorship programs, loans, and seminars to support women entrepreneurs.
